Home Town: Gilford, New Hampshire
Current Location: Craftsbury, Vermont  

Did you know….?

My dream vacation spot is…. New Zealand 
When traveling, the thing I miss the most is….. my dog, Gunner 
My favorite meal is…. grass fed, bacon cheeseburger 
I never leave the house without….. my leatherman 
If I were not an athlete, I would…..have a cattle, pig and turkey farm

MORE ON ALEX: Alex started skiing in middle school for Gilford Middle School and Gunstock Nordic. He was a multiple-time junior national champion and represented the U.S. at the J1 Scandinavian Cup and the Junior World Chmpionships. He went on to ski for the University of Vermont and helped the Catamounts win a NCAA Championship. Alex studied Ecological Agriculture at UVM. He joined the Craftsbury Green Racing Project in May of 2012 and raced his first biathlon races in February 2015. Following the 15/16 season, he was named to U.S. Biathlon's development program. He raced his first IBU Cup in Martell, Italy, in January of 2017.

Quick Facts
  • Position: Second 
  • Brother, Tom, is also a member of the U.S. national team
  • Two-time national junior champion
Biographical Information 

Allison Howell was born in New Jersey and started curling alongside her brother Tom at age 4. 

Like her brother Tom, she was a two-time national junior champion.

Howell, as a member of Team Cousins, was the 2025 national runner-up and qualified for her second U.S. Olympic Trials. 

Howell graduated from the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee in 2022 with a degree in nutritional science and is currently a graduate student at UWM, aiming to become a registered dietitian.

Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championship, Aberdeen, 2018 (Team - Women)
  • World Junior Championship, Pyeongchang, 2017 (Team - Women)

  • Position: Third
  • Speciality: Men's Curling
  • Introduced to curling when his parents would slide him down the ice in a car seat while they were practicing at their local curling club
  • Hobbies: hiking, golfing, and boating
Biographical Information 

Tom Howell grew up in New Jersey and was introduced to the sport when his parents, Bob and Rachel, would slide him down the ice in a car seat while they were practicing at their local curling club.

At age 17, Howell participated for Team USA in curling at the Winter Youth Olympic Games Innsbruck 2012, competing in two events (mixed doubles and mixed team). Four years later, Howell would win a silver medal at the world junior men's curling championships on a team with two of his current teammates, Korey Dropkin and Mark Fenner. 

 

Howell has been a national champion twice in men's curling, earning the right to represent the United States at the 2022 and 2025 world championships, both times with Dropkin as the team's skip.  Howell has been on the runner-up team at two straight U.S. Olympic Team Trials in men's curling, in 2017 and 2021. 

 

Howell graduated from Marquette University with a degree in economics and business. He currently resides in Wisconsin, working as a financial advisor, balancing his job with the demands of being an elite curler. In his free time, he enjoys spending time with his wife, Hanna, and Australian Shepherd dog, Oslo, as well as hiking, golfing, boating and traveling.

  • Top finish: 4th – 2022 (Team - Men)
